page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable instance type based on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 8269CY cores running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is generally weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1303.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1039.0, both falling in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance reaches average levels, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 9658.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4827.0. Memory bandwidth remains average, with read-only large block speeds of 61.66 GB/sec and write speeds of 33.94 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation model, this instance is designed for workloads with fluctuating resource demands. It is highly cost-efficient for applications that remain idle or run at low baseline levels but require occasional performance bursts. The lack of local storage and baseline network bandwidth means storage and network operations must be handled externally. It is well-suited for light database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, but is poorly suited for intensive single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 server instance featuring 8 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results show weak single-core performance, but multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput achieve average tier results. The burstable CPU allocation makes this instance a qualitatively cost-effective choice for workloads with variable CPU utilization, such as development environments, light database operations, and static web serving, while avoiding the higher costs of dedicated, non-burstable hardware.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t6-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8269CY CPU. It offers average multi-core performance for variable workloads.

tagline: A burstable x86_64 instance with eight cores and balanced memory, optimized for variable workloads and cost-effective multi-core processing.

Burstable CPU allocation model for variable, non-sustained workloads
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks
